resources.name: Veterans Crisis Line
resources.description: Free, confidential 24/7 crisis support for veterans, service members, and their families, staffed by trained responders (many are veterans themselves). Available even if you are not enrolled in VA benefits or health care.

resources.name: 1-800-MyVA411 (VA.gov Benefits & Services)
resources.description: VA's single front-door phone line and website for all VA benefits and services, including disability compensation, pension, education, home loans, health care enrollment, and burial benefits. Press 0 for a live agent.

resources.name: San Francisco VA Health Care System (SFVAHCS)
resources.description: The main VA medical center serving Bay Area veterans, offering primary care, mental health, PTSD treatment, specialty care, and emergency services. Enrollment in VA health care connects veterans to ongoing clinical support.
resources.hours: Medical center open 24/7; outpatient clinics generally Mon-Fri daytime hours (call to confirm)

resources.name: San Francisco Vet Center
resources.description: Community-based VA center offering free, confidential counseling for combat veterans and their families, including readjustment counseling, PTSD support, military sexual trauma care, and bereavement support. Vet Centers are separate from VA medical records.
resources.address: 850 Battery St, Second Floor, San Francisco, CA 94111
resources.hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 a.m.-4:30 p.m.; some non-traditional hours (call ahead)

resources.name: National Call Center for Homeless Veterans
resources.description: Free, confidential 24/7 line connecting veterans who are homeless or at risk of homelessness (and their families or supporters) with VA housing programs, case managers, and local resources.

resources.name: National Center for PTSD / PTSD Coach
resources.description: VA's authoritative source on PTSD, offering free educational resources plus the PTSD Coach mobile app and PTSD Coach Online with self-help tools to learn about and manage trauma symptoms. Not a substitute for professional care.
resources.hours: App and website available anytime; info line during business hours

resources.name: Wounded Warrior Project - Resource Center
resources.description: Nonprofit connecting wounded, ill, and injured post-9/11 veterans and their families to free programs in mental health, peer support, benefits guidance, physical health, and career counseling.

resources.name: Swords to Plowshares
resources.description: Bay Area nonprofit founded by Vietnam veterans in 1974, providing free housing, employment, benefits advocacy, and legal services to thousands of local veterans each year.
resources.hours: Mon 9:00 a.m.-7:00 p.m.; Tue-Thu 8:00 a.m.-7:00 p.m.; Fri 8:00 a.m.-6:00 p.m. (call to confirm)

resources.name: Team Red, White & Blue (Team RWB)
resources.description: National veterans nonprofit that enriches veterans' health and well-being through local and virtual physical activity, social events, and community connection, with active chapters including the Bay Area.
resources.hours: Community events scheduled locally; see chapter listings online

resources.name: Hire Heroes USA
resources.description: Nonprofit providing free, personalized job-search assistance to transitioning service members, veterans, and military spouses, including resume help, career coaching, and employer connections.
resources.hours: Mon-Fri business hours (services delivered virtually nationwide)

resources.name: Military OneSource
resources.description: Department of Defense 24/7 gateway offering confidential non-medical counseling, financial and spouse-career consultations, and resources for service members, families, and survivors.

resources.name: SAMHSA National Helpline
resources.description: Free, confidential 24/7 treatment referral and information service (English and Spanish) for individuals and families facing mental health and/or substance use disorders, with referrals to local treatment and support groups.

resources.name: VA Palo Alto Health Care System
resources.description: Major South Bay VA medical center providing comprehensive care including mental health, PTSD and polytrauma rehabilitation, primary care, and specialty services for Peninsula and South Bay veterans.
resources.hours: Medical center open 24/7; clinics vary (call to confirm)

resources.name: VA Caregiver Support Line
resources.description: VA line for caregivers, family, and friends of veterans, offering information, referrals, and connection to local Caregiver Support Program coordinators for counseling and support services.
resources.hours: Mon-Fri 8:00 a.m.-11:00 p.m. ET; Sat 10:30 a.m.-6:00 p.m. ET

resources.name: DAV (Disabled American Veterans)
resources.description: Nonprofit with National Service Officers who provide free help filing and appealing VA disability, pension, health care, and other benefit claims. Membership is not required, and services are free.
resources.hours: Mon-Fri business hours (nationwide network of offices)
